localContext: Laurelton's mix of single-family homes, Tudor-style houses near Merrick Boulevard, and properties surrounding Francis Lewis Park often experience spider activity, especially in basements, attics, and garages. The area's proximity to green spaces and older housing stock creates ideal conditions for common house spiders, cellar spiders, and occasionally wolf spiders seeking shelter indoors.

faqs.question: What types of spiders are common in Laurelton homes?
faqs.answer: Laurelton residents most commonly encounter house spiders, cellar spiders (daddy longlegs), wolf spiders, and jumping spiders. While most spiders in our area are harmless, they can be unsettling and indicate other pest problems since spiders feed on insects. Our treatment targets all spider species and their food sources.

faqs.question: Are your spider treatments safe for children and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products and methods that are safe for families and pets when applied by our trained professionals. We'll provide clear instructions on any brief precautions needed during treatment, and you can typically return to treated areas within a few hours.
